name: New-Fashioned Cabbage Rolls
recipeIngredient: Sweet vermouth or apple cider (1/2 cup)
Dried fruits (such as raisins, apples, apricots, peaches, or pears) (1/2 cup, chopped)
recipeInstructions:
1. Sauce: Heat oil in a medium saucepan. Add onions and sauté until transparent. Add tomatoes, vermouth or cider, lemon juice, and tomato paste. Cook over medium heat for 15 minutes. Add salt and pepper to taste. Makes 4 cups.
2. Filling: Heat oil in a large skillet. Add onion and garlic and sauté until onions are transparent. Add mushrooms and cook briefly, about 4 minutes. Stir in the rice, paprika, dill seed, and dried fruit. Cook until the fruit softens, about 10 minutes. Stir in walnuts and remove from heat.
3. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Core the cabbage and place it in a large pot of boiling water. Parboil for 5 minutes. Meanwhile, spread about 1/3 of the tomato sauce in the bottom of a large casserole dish.
4. Remove cabbage from the pan, drain, and cool slightly. Carefully peel off 16 cabbage leaves. If inner leaves are too stiff, place them back in the boiling water for 1 to 2 minutes until pliable. Put about 2-3 tablespoons of filling in the center of each cabbage leaf.
5. Roll up the leaf, tucking in the sides as you roll, and place each packet seam-side down in a layer in the casserole dish. Cover the layer with sauce. Continue rolling, layering, and adding sauce until you've used up the filling.
6. Cover and bake for 45 to 60 minutes, until fully cooked. Remove from oven and let stand about 30 minutes to allow flavors to develop. Serves 6.
Per serving: 283 calories; 6g protein; 10g fat; 36g carbohydrates; 0 cholesterol; 214mg sodium; 4g fiber; vegan/Vegetarian Times, Nov 93/MM by DEEANNE
